frameworks.name: intrinsic neural timescales
frameworks.one_line: autocorrelation magnitude of intrinsic signals indexes how long information persists locally

frameworks.name: resting-state fMRI functional dynamics
frameworks.one_line: local temporal autocorrelation of resting BOLD characterises regional information storage

frameworks.name: structure-function coupling
frameworks.one_line: intrinsic timescale linked to local grey matter volume across regions

frameworks.name: neurodevelopmental account of autism
frameworks.one_line: atypical local dynamics framed within developmental trajectory of autism

core_question: Do intrinsic neural timescales differ in autism and relate to its behavioural and cognitive symptoms?
key_assumptions: autocorrelation of resting signals indexes local information storage duration
local intrinsic dynamics reflect region-specific function
atypical regional dynamics can map onto higher-order cognitive symptoms
manipulations: group comparison: high-functioning autistic adults vs controls
replication across two independent cross-sectional datasets
key_findings: shorter intrinsic timescales in sensory/visual cortices in autism
longer intrinsic timescale in the right caudate in autism
sensory/visual timescale shortening correlates with autism severity; caudate lengthening correlates with cognitive rigidity
intrinsic timescale correlates with local grey matter volume
interpretations: local functional and structural atypicality links to higher-order cognitive symptoms in autism
regionally specific intrinsic dynamics underlie distinct symptom dimensions
